invariants: Headwear must follow the provided reference identity when reference-driven
Headwear must sit naturally on the head
Hairline and facial identity must not be distorted
Garment neckline and primary garment pattern zones must remain visible unless explicitly allowed otherwise
Headwear must remain realistic and physically believable
allowed_transforms: Minor scale adaptation for head proportion realism
Natural fold adaptation consistent with pose
Micro shadow adjustments around contact zones
forbidden: Any identity transfer from reference person to subject
Any hijab-like reinterpretation unless explicitly requested
Any occlusion of key garment zones when not allowed
Any floating or physically implausible placement
Any stylization that reduces catalog realism
placement_rules: Accessory must anchor naturally to the head or hair
No implausible clipping into skull, forehead, or hair volume
Back tie or knot behavior must follow the requested styling logic when applicable
coverage_rules: Do not cover garment neckline unless explicitly allowed
Do not block primary garment print zones
Do not hide facial identity-critical regions unless explicitly requested
